texto: For brick and mortar breed filth and crime,With a pulse of evil that throbs and beats;And men are whithered before their primeBy the curse paved in with the lanes and streets.And lungs are poisoned and shoulders bowed, In the smothering reek of mill and mine;And death stalks in on the struggling crowd—But he shuns the shadow of the oak and pine
explicacao: Orientação direta de George Washington Sears sobre temas ligados a familia. A afirmação “For brick and mortar breed filth and crime,With a pulse of evil that throbs and beats;And men are…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, camping, city, útil em momentos de luto e city.
